channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Yuri visual novel Black Lily’s Tale coming to Switch on October 29 in Japan
item.content:encoded:
• Switch

Currently available for PC, iOS, and Android.

Kadokawa Game Linkage will release a Switch version of 1000-REKA-developed “dystopian yuri visual novel” Black Lily’s Tale on October 29 for 4,378 yen, the publisher announced.

Black Lily’s Tale first launched for PC via Steam on October 24, 2023 in Japan, followed by iOS and Android worldwide via Crunchyroll Game Vault, and PC worldwide on October 24, 2025.

Despite the PC version supporting English, Japanese, Korean, and Simplified Chinese, the Switch version will only support Japanese as a language option.

The Switch version will include all previously released additional scenarios, as well as the additional scenario “The Day We First Crossed the Boundary,” which follows Hana Sasamori and Itsuki Omiya after the main story.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Perhaps it was the curse of a black lily that bloomed too early in the winter… Before graduation, a girl falls in love with another girl… But her pure feelings are torn apart by a “loop of tragedy.” By “interrupting choices” and “typing in words”, you will weave her feelings into the story.

Story

“Maybe that was the early-blooming black lily’s curse…”

Eighteen years have passed since the containment of the threatening virus CLII, which decimated over half of the minors in the population.

Although people have returned to their normal lives, a mark was left on society.

Hana Sasamori is a third-year student at the Naeshiro Private High School in Sapporo.

She is a rather quiet girl, but inside, she feels restless.

Just before graduation, Hana finds herself reliving the same day over and over.

“That’s right… I’m in love with her.”

More than anything, Hana wishes for the happiness of Itsuki, who came to her help in the past.

There is little she wouldn’t do for her.

Hana thinks…

“She probably doesn’t want me to hold onto such a feeling…”

Being an adolescent, she thought it was just a phase that deserved to be suppressed.

Nevertheless, together with that realization came a curse.

This is a story of love and malediction, of persistence and sorrow, a story set in the meandering space between two people looking for each other.

Key Features

  • A yuri adventure featuring Fukahire’s beautifully nostalgic illustrations.
  • A yuri coming-of-age story that explores the struggles of a girl falling in love with another girl.
  • Make choices at your own pace, type in keywords, and shape the fate of the story.
  • Fully voiced characters.
  • Beautiful animated character sprites bring the story to life.
  • Supports Japanese and English languages.
  • Features a story flowchart to help you navigate the narrative.

Concept

  • Conflict x Adolescence – A story about “liking” and “cursing” in a world where girls are afraid to even say they like each other.
    • This work is just a story about a girl who loves a girl. However, a pure love triangle with a boy is born in this story, which vividly depicts the subtleties of the conflicted and turbulent adolescent mind.
    • It speaks to the sense of entrapment that you feel even when you are not being blamed by anyone. A single word can destroy your relationships and memories.
    • This is a story of sweet sorrow love and a curse that makes you want to cheer for the girl.
  • Tragedy x Interrupt – Overcoming the “tragedy” of March by “interrupting the choices.”
    • The main character “Hana Sasamori” is caught in a loop with a tragic ending in each chapter.
    • Unlike ordinary games, this title does not force you to make choices!
    • To escape the tragedy, you must “interrupt choices and change your fate” during specific conversations through your intervening will.
  • Guess x Type – “Guess” and “type” to connect to a new fate.
    • Next, having interrupted the choice, you must actually enter an alternate “word” by typing it into the prompt. By “guessing” correctly and inputting a word that changes their fate in the story, you may avoid tragedy and create a new fate.
    • You will surely want to use the above features once you bear witness Hana’s single-mindedness. You can intervene in the yuri and lead both girls to happiness by using your words to bring them together.”

*To clarify the concept, this work incorporates elements of a pure and adolescent love triangle. However, there is no intention or malice on the part of a boy to “interfere” with the relationship between two girls.

Characters

  • Hana Sasamori (voiced by Chikano Ibuki) – “I just want her to be happy…” Hana Sasamori is the game’s main character and relies on the player’s decisions. Although she may appear calm and reserved, her inner self is rather tempestuous. That being said, she doesn’t usually assert her opinion, and can easily get flustered. For this reason, she tends to show unexpected reactions, but this aspect of her personality is also appreciated by Itsuki and Aoi. Having a sense of gratitude toward Itsuki for her help during junior high school, she is determined to return the favor somehow. Hana hoped that her lonesomeness would fade away in the time before graduation, but that was not withstanding a tragic event that would befall her dearest Itsuki.
  • Itsuki Omiya (voiced by Miho Ichidera) – “Hold on. Let me expel your worries…” Itsuki is a former member of the track team who can get along with anyone, with a bubbly and slightly boyish temperament. In the span of a minute, she can show a wide variety of emotions, from smiling to pouting, laughing to crying. Hana finds these mood changes enticing. “She asked Hana for help with her studies, and it was decided that they would go to the same university. However, as graduation drew near, a secret she had been keeping from everyone grew heavier on her mind. Then one day, she exclaimed, Let’s write all that is left undone. Or the word cards!”” She had brought a set of such cards with her.” It always seemed as if nothing could alter her joviality.
  • Aoi Nishiki (voiced by Aoi Kureha) – “I’d rather say you’re my ‘little sister,’ you know…” Cool-headed and serene, she spends much of her time with Hana and Itsuki. She often finds herself acting as a coordinator between them, as Hana is so indecisive, and Itsuki so exuberant. This role gives her an adult air, yet she enjoys poking fun at others, especially Hana and Itsuki whose exchanges never fail to amuse her.
    A shark movie freak, she gets inexplicably excited about this movie genre. She lives next door to Ai Isshiki, with whom she has had a sisterly relationship since they were kids.
  • Ai Isshiki (voiced by Chisato Satsuki) – “I’m Ai Isshiki! Aoi’s neighbor and childhood friend!” A talkative and friendly young girl. She has been chatty and restless since she was a kid, to the point of making those around her uneasy. With her emotions on full display, people can’t help but grow fond of her transparent way of being. As an only child, she became close to her neighbor and child-hood friend Aoi, viewing her as an older sister.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/yuri-visual-novel-black-lilys-tale-coming-to-switch-on-october-29-in-japan
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Bungie announces layoffs, reorganization
item.content:encoded:
• PlayStation

"These changes are necessary to best position the studio now and for the future," Bungie says.

Bungie has announced a “reduction in force” as it ‘reorganizes’ to “to best position the studio now and for the future” following its final content update for Destiny 2.

The number of employees laid off was not announced, and Bungie said it will share more about its future at a later date.

Here is the full message from Bungie below:

With great sadness, we are announcing a reduction in force as we reorganize Bungie.

As the leaders of Bungie, past and present, we recognize Destiny 2 fell short of expectations these past several years. Following our final content update to Destiny 2, and with our future projects still in early incubation, we unfortunately could not continue operating at our previous size.

We know this decision has a profound impact on the people affected, their families, friends, and teammates. While these changes are necessary to best position the studio now and for the future, that does not lessen the difficulty of this moment or the impact it has on those affected.

Later, we will share more about that future with you all but today is not that day.

Today, we wish to extend our gratitude and compassion to every member of the Bungie team who has been impacted and to those who remain.

We hope you will do the same.

—Bungie

Update 10:40 a.m.: Sony Interactive Entertainment Studio Business Group CEO Hermen Hulst has shared the following internal email regarding today’s Bungie layoffs, which confirms additional reductions across Sony Interactive Entertainment teams that support Bungie’s operations:

Dear Team,

Today I want to share a difficult update regarding Bungie.

We have made the decision to reduce Bungie’s workforce, affecting a significant number of employees, including most of the Destiny team and some Marathon team members. There are also reductions across [Sony Interactive Entertainment] teams that support Bungie’s operations. Those impacted at Bungie and within [Sony Interactive Entertainment] are being informed today.

This is painful news, especially for talented colleagues whose roles have been eliminated. This decision was made only after extensive discussion and careful consideration, and I want to provide some context on how we arrived here. Over the past several months, together with Bungie leadership, we reviewed the studio’s long-term direction, development priorities, resource needs, and role within our broader portfolio strategy. We explored multiple alternatives before concluding that a reduction was necessary to align the studio’s resources with its current priorities and long-term goals.

As Bungie recently shared in its Destiny 2 update, the studio has begun a new journey following the release of the game’s final live-service content update. What Bungie has accomplished with Destiny over the past decade has been truly remarkable. The franchise has left a lasting mark on players and the industry, and everyone who contributed to its success should be proud of what they helped create.

Marathon remains an important part of our portfolio, and we will continue to support the team as they build on the strong foundation established in Season 1 and 2, and as they work on incubation efforts for future projects. While it’s too early to discuss, we are encouraged by the creativity and opportunities that lie ahead.

Our immediate priority is supporting affected employees through this transition. We are providing transition support and, where possible, working to identify opportunities across [Sony Interactive Entertainment] and our global network of studios.

I want to sincerely thank every affected employee for their hard work, creativity, and contributions to Bungie, [Sony Interactive Entertainment], and the broader gaming community.

I know today’s news is deeply difficult not only for those leaving, but for those colleagues and friends that remain. Please take the time you need to process this news and support one another.

Thank you for your resilience and continued support during this difficult time.

Hermen Hulst
CEO, Studio Business Group



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/bungie-announces-layoffs-reorganization
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
